# ZCAM SDK

## Docs

- [Architecture](/technical-docs/architecture): The ZCAM SDK allows an integrating app to:
- [FAQs](/technical-docs/faq): *Does ZCAM work on Android?*
- [Security](/technical-docs/security): A verified zphoto provides the following guarantees:
- [ZK Proof Details](/technical-docs/zkproof-details): The SP1 program proves the following:
- [Capture SDK](/sdk/capture): The Capture SDK (`react-native-zcam1-capture`) takes photos and produces C2PA-signed assets bound to hardware-backed integrity signals.
- [Picker SDK](/sdk/picker): The Picker SDK (`react-native-zcam1-picker`) provides an image picker component with built-in authenticity status badges.
- [Prove SDK](/sdk/prove): The Prove SDK (`react-native-zcam1-prove`) generates zero-knowledge proofs for C2PA-signed images and embeds them back into the photo.
- [Verify SDK](/sdk/verify): The Verify SDK (`react-native-zcam1-verify`) validates the authenticity of C2PA-signed photos with embedded proofs.
- [ZCAM vs Other Approaches](/overview/comparisons_to_other_approaches): Numerous technologies exist for image authenticity and provenance. These solutions address different problem spaces and offer varying trade-offs.
- [Example Use Cases](/overview/example_usecases): Below are two real-world examples demonstrating how the ZCAM SDK can be integrated to solve verification challenges. Each example highlights a distinct verification pattern: **end-user verification** (public-facing) versus **internal verification** (backend processing).
- [How it Works](/overview/how_it_works): ZCAM verifies photos through a combination of hardware-backed attestation, cryptographic signatures, and optional zero-knowledge proofs.
- [Introduction](/overview/introduction): Today, with the rise of AI-generated imagery, widespread editing tools, and growing incentives to deceive, image integrity is more important than ever.
- [Installation](/getting-started/installation): The ZCAM SDK packages include Rust bindings built via UniFFI. You need Rust installed with iOS targets:
- [Quickstart](/getting-started/quickstart): This guide demonstrates a complete flow: capturing a photo, generating a proof, and verifying it.
- [Editing a ZCAM Photo](/c2pa-ecosystem/editing_zcam_photos): All zPhotos captured with ZCAM are C2PA-compatible, so any C2PA-aware editor can preserve the original capture manifest while adding an edit manifest.
- [Hardware Camera Support](/c2pa-ecosystem/hardware_camera_support): We are beginning to see camera companies roll out C2PA-supported cameras. Leica was the first camera company to really lean into C2PA starting in late 2023, with other companies adding support since then.
